mnemonics: Use CATCH to remember Collection Interfaces: Collection (root), List, Queue, Deque, Set, SortedSet, NavigableSet, Map, SortedMap, NavigableMap
keyPoints: The Collections Framework provides a unified architecture for representing and manipulating collections, enabling them to be manipulated independently of their implementation details.
Key interfaces include Collection, List, Set, Queue, and Map, which define the core functionality for different types of collections.
The framework includes utility classes like Collections and Arrays that provide useful algorithms (e.g., sorting, searching) and static methods for operations on collections.
commonMistakes: Using a raw type (e.g., ArrayList instead of ArrayList<String>) which bypasses generic type checks and can lead to ClassCastExceptions at runtime.
Assuming that implementations like HashSet or HashMap maintain insertion order; they do not guarantee any specific order of elements.
